description: The figure presents a three-part validation of a dynamic recrystallization (DRX) grain size prediction model for low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel subjected to hot deformation. Subplot (a) displays experimental grain size versus deformation temperature (900–1150°C) at constant strain rates of 0.01, 0.1, 1, and 10 s⁻¹, demonstrating that grain size increases monotonically with temperature while decreasing with increasing strain rate, with values ranging from approximately 4 μm (at 900°C/10 s⁻¹) to 75 μm (at 1150°C/0.01 s⁻¹). Subplot (b) compares predicted versus experimental grain sizes, showing strong linear correlation with a correlation coefficient R = 0.996 and mean relative error MRE = 4.53%, with data points largely falling within the ±10% deviation bands indicated by red lines. Subplot (c) illustrates the distribution of absolute prediction error versus experimental grain size, confirming that most prediction errors remain below 3 μm as marked by the dashed blue line. These results collectively validate the accuracy and reliability of the established DRX grain size model for predicting microstructural evolution during hot forging of this steel.
